mechanism_narrative: When retail sales trend upward over the prior 3 months, expected corporate revenue growth is revised higher and cyclically sensitive earnings expectations improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 529766 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month change in RSAFS has a nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if positive RSAFS trends are followed by positive returns in less than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment after a low-unemployment expansion signals that labor demand is weakening and recession risk is being repriced, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 6-month minimum by at least 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 3.6 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered average returns are not below non-triggered average returns by at least 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the easing is not accompanied by a contemporaneous unemployment shock.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below its 6-month moving average by at least 0.25 percentage points while UNRATE is not up more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 1.55 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if easing-without-labor-stress triggers have a 60-day mean excess return less than or equal to zero, or if adding the UNRATE condition does not improve hit rate versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals falling real activity and weaker operating leverage for manufacturers and cyclicals, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average and its 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 109.2026 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the triggered mean return is not lower than the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts reflects improving household formation, construction activity, and durable-goods demand, supporting earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month maximum
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1608 as of two months before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout triggers have a 60-day S&P 500 hit rate below 50%, or if their average 60-day return is not at least 25 basis points above the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices while policy rates do not fall raises real-income pressure and reduces scope for monetary support, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as inflation risk is repriced.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS is flat or higher over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 258.82 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if this joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger has nonnegative average 5-day forward returns, or if the interaction term does not improve directional accuracy versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth indicates expanding aggregate wage income and demand, supporting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and above its trailing 12-month median change
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 152186 as of one month before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if above-median PAYEMS growth is not associated with a positive 20-day forward return mean, or if the sign accuracy falls below 50% over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth paired with a rising federal funds rate identifies a late-cycle overheating regime in which discount-rate pressure dominates cash-flow growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive while FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 19219.767 as of four months before 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-positive and FEDFUNDS-rising interaction has positive 60-day average S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.
